summary: Warm, well‑recorded soundboard capture of a classic Sept 15 1985 show highlighted by an unforgettable Scarlet > Fire and solid U.S. Blues set.
review: The set‑2 recording is a clean soundboard source that delivers the warm tube‑tone ambience the Dead were famous for in ’85, with plenty of praise for Jerry’s soaring leads on Scarlet Begonias > Fire and the tight groove on Samson & Delilah.  The performance is strong overall – U.S. Blues, Truckin’, She Belongs to Me and a high‑energy Satisfaction round out a set that many fans describe as “primo ’85” despite a few sloppy moments such as an underwhelming Comes a Time.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, emphasizing excellent soundboard quality and standout performances; only minor criticisms (sloppy moments) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as warm, clear and well‑balanced with a pleasant tube‑fuzz tone; several reviewers note it is “reasonably good quality board recording” and “great sound”.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note an experimental yet cohesive flow – the Twilight Zone tuning segues into a mellow Scarlet‑Fire opener, followed by tight jams and a high‑energy U.S. Blues finish that keeps the momentum rolling.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for “sweetness”, soaring leads on Scarlet > Fire, inventive licks on Samson, and warm, froggy vocal tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for his “first notes” on Satisfaction and laying down a solid low‑end “down comforter”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard textures described as “sepia bubbles” adding warmth to the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and Mickey) receive nods for high‑jinks and driving the U.S. Blues groove.
